verses.text: I came not with 'excellency of speech,' 'for I determined not to know any thing among you, save Jesus Christ, and him crucified.' One theme. The crucified Christ was his whole message.
I came not with 'excellency of speech,' 'for I determined not to know any thing among you, save Jesus Christ, and him crucified.' One theme. The crucified Christ was his whole message.
I was with you 'in weakness, and in fear,' that your faith stand 'in the power of God.' Not by eloquence. Faith rests on God's power, not man's words.
I was with you 'in weakness, and in fear,' that your faith stand 'in the power of God.' Not by eloquence. Faith rests on God's power, not man's words.
I was with you 'in weakness, and in fear,' that your faith stand 'in the power of God.' Not by eloquence. Faith rests on God's power, not man's words.
We speak hidden wisdom, which 'none of the princes of this world knew.' Secret wisdom. The rulers crucified the Lord of glory in ignorance.
We speak hidden wisdom, which 'none of the princes of this world knew.' Secret wisdom. The rulers crucified the Lord of glory in ignorance.
We speak hidden wisdom, which 'none of the princes of this world knew.' Secret wisdom. The rulers crucified the Lord of glory in ignorance.
'Eye hath not seen... the things which God hath prepared'; He hath revealed them 'by his Spirit.' Revealed. The Spirit unveils what man cannot find out.
'Eye hath not seen... the things which God hath prepared'; He hath revealed them 'by his Spirit.' Revealed. The Spirit unveils what man cannot find out.
The Spirit searches 'the deep things of God'; we have received Him, to know 'the things that are freely given.' Spirit-taught. Only God's Spirit makes known God's gifts.
The Spirit searches 'the deep things of God'; we have received Him, to know 'the things that are freely given.' Spirit-taught. Only God's Spirit makes known God's gifts.
The Spirit searches 'the deep things of God'; we have received Him, to know 'the things that are freely given.' Spirit-taught. Only God's Spirit makes known God's gifts.
'The natural man receiveth not the things of the Spirit'; they are 'spiritually discerned'; we have the mind of Christ. Spiritual sight. The unrenewed cannot grasp spiritual truth.
